title: Neonatal & Pediatrics
subtitle: Care for Youngest Patients
description: Focused on specialized care for infants, children and adolescents with family-centered approaches.

detail.mission_text: To prepare compassionate neonatal and pediatric nurses who can provide developmentally appropriate care for infants, children, and adolescents while supporting families through evidence-based practice.
detail.intro: The Department of Neonatal and Pediatrics Nursing focuses on the specialized healthcare needs of children from birth through adolescence. Our program emphasizes developmental considerations, family-centered care, and the unique physiological and psychological aspects of pediatric nursing.

areas.title: Neonatal Nursing
areas.description: Specialized care for newborns, particularly premature infants and those with medical complications requiring intensive monitoring.

areas.title: Pediatric Nursing
areas.description: Comprehensive care for children from infancy through adolescence, focusing on growth, development, and age-appropriate interventions.

areas.title: Pediatric Critical Care
areas.description: Advanced care for critically ill children, including pediatric intensive care and emergency pediatric interventions.

areas.title: Family-Centered Care
areas.description: Holistic approach involving parents and families in care planning, education, and support throughout the child's healthcare journey.

training.title: Neonatal Simulation
training.description: Advanced neonatal simulators for premature infant care, neonatal resuscitation, and intensive care skill development.

training.title: Pediatric Clinical Skills
training.description: Hands-on training in pediatric assessment, medication administration, and developmental care across all pediatric age groups.

training.title: Family Support Training
training.description: Training in family education, counseling, and support strategies to enhance family-centered care delivery.
